description:
WHY: release-plz.toml configures custom_minor_increment_regex = '\[minor\]' so a bracketed [minor] marker anywhere in a commit message bumps the minor version. The regex is too loose: bl-313a's own commit subject ('Auto-derive minor/major bumps from [minor]/[major] markers [bl-313a]') contains the literal string [minor] embedded in prose ('[minor]/[major] markers'), and self-matched. release-plz then proposed v0.5.0 for the post-0.4.0 window even though no commit intended a minor bump. v0.4.0 was already hand-edited away from release-plz's default; recurring that workflow defeats the point of bl-313a.

SHAPE: keep the marker convention, tighten the regex to '(^|\s)\[minor\](\s|$)' and '(^|\s)\[major\](\s|$)' so the bracketed marker must sit at a word-boundary — start-of-string, preceded by whitespace, followed by whitespace, or end-of-string. Prose like '[minor]/[major]' (slash-joined, no surrounding whitespace) no longer matches; a real marker line like 'Adds the X feature [minor] [bl-xxxx]' still matches. Update the toml comment to reflect the boundary requirement.

TEST: tests/release_plz_marker.rs (or a unit test if release-plz config validates these) exercises three strings against each regex: (a) 'foo [minor] bar' matches; (b) '[minor]/[major] markers' does NOT match; (c) 'foo [minor]' (trailing end-of-string) matches. Same for [major].

OBSERVED: PR #30 (chore: release v0.5.0) is the immediate symptom — needs to be regenerated as v0.4.1 once this lands.
title: release-plz [minor]/[major] regex must require standalone tokens
